rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens & To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 405.30 Et Seq. & 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2019 line . CROSS COMPLAINANT CARLOS MARTINEZ Notice Of Motion & Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens & To Enforce Settlement Pursuant To Ccp Section 405.30 Et Seq. & 664.6 Hearing Required to address whether Alma Campos is presently able to close the purchase. If Alma is able to close within 7 days of the hearing, the Court is inclined to deny the motion. If Alma is not able to close the Court is inclined (1) to grant the motion to expunge lis pendens and to deny the attorney's fees due to substantial justification re: delay in providing necessary information; and (2) to deny the motion for entry of judgment as requested. Moving party failed to identify any provision of the settlement agreement that would entitle him to a judgment "confirming that Carlos Martinez is the owner on fees simple of the Property, a determination that there is no right to title therein in Milagro Campos; and for money damages." Specifically, no provision of the settlement agreement is identifies listing the content and the nature of a judgment in case of failure to comply with any provisions of the settlement agreement. The Court notes that paragraph 6 of the Agreement suggests that Carlos at most may be entitled to 50% interest. However, the Court will not be making any such determinations at this time. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN MANHAN ET AL VS. SHANNON GALLAGHER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2019 line . DEFENDANT SHANNON GALLAGHER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Or To Set Aside Sanction Orders Or To Rehear Plaintiffs' Motions For Sanctions, Or, In The Alternative For Excusable Neglect GRANTED. The Court has the power to control in furtherance of justice its process and orders to make them conform to law and justice. Moving party establishes valid grounds for a motion for reconsideration. Opposing party fails to provide any opposition on the merits. To the extent a procedural objection is made, it is overruled. This Court's orders of March 20, 2019 and April 2, 2019 are null, void and unenforceable. A copy of this order, along with a copy of the papers in support of this motion shall be served on the state bar by the clerk of the court re: Plaintiff counsel of record. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN F. LEE VS. DANIEL EVERETT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 23, 2019 line . DEFENDANT DANIEL EVERETT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant improperly raises issues of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer (e.g. property/tenancy being commercial or residential) and fails to cite any authority for the proposition that master tenant has to allege that it is action "at the bequest of the landlord." Allegations regarding service of the operative Notice are sufficient, see paragraph 6, 8, attachment 12, attachment 15 and Ex. 2. = (501/CFH)
